title: Repo analysis skill — implementation
description: Build /repo-analysis skill from approved deep-plan (24 decisions, 9 steps). Automated health assessment + conversational value extraction. Quick Scan (API-only default) → Standard (clone + agents) → Deep (temporal). Plan at .planning/repo-analysis-skill/PLAN.md.
progress: Implemented Session #257. SKILL.md (377 lines) + REFERENCE.md (850 lines). 3 tiers, 45 dimensions, routing menu. Verified in PR #489 R1 integrity sweep. v2.0 upgrade Session #261: adoption assessment, extraction persistence, schema alignment.

title: Dev dashboard — 6-tab command center
description: Build developer dashboard with 6 tabs for operational visibility. XL effort, multi-session. Plan at .planning/dev-dashboard/PLAN.md.

title: debt-runner expansion — /deep-plan
description: Research done, needs implementation plan via /deep-plan. Gates plan-orchestration Waves 2-3.
progress: Research done. Blocked on T2 (dev dashboard) for data visibility.

title: Multi-layer memory — execute research findings
description: 30-agent research complete (128 claims, 3 waves). Enhance existing autoMemoryDirectory system, don't replace. Research state file exists at home locale, recover and execute.

title: Worktree management — .claude/ syncing + parallel instance strategies
description: Two related problems: (1) Extend superpowers:using-git-worktrees with .claude/ directory syncing (skills, agents, rules, state). Use .worktrees/ project-local approach, add gitignore + dep install + test baseline. (2) Strategies for running two CC instances on same repo simultaneously — branches share working dir so checkout affects both, worktrees share .claude/ causing hook/state/script interference. Evaluate: separate clones, worktree with .claude isolation, or other approaches.
progress: Brainstorm complete (6 directions, paused at Phase 2). Testing Direction F (claude -w) now. Artifacts at .planning/worktree-management/. Key discovery: user never used native -w flag — conversational worktree creation doesn't give process isolation.

title: Plan orchestration — Waves 2-3
description: SWS CANON + M1.6 features. Blocked on debt-runner expansion (T3). Wave 1 (Steps 1-10) complete.

title: SWS CANON enforcement (Step 12)
description: 6-10 session effort. Part of plan orchestration Wave 2. Blocked on T6.

title: Custom agents implementation
description: Plan at .planning/custom-agents/PLAN.md. Includes contrarian/OTB agents (Steps 15-19).
progress: All 25 steps complete. P0 infrastructure, P1 consolidation, P2 pipeline agents, validation all done. Session #255.

title: Agent stalling on large input — investigation
description: Agents reading 16+ findings files for synthesis silently stall (context exhaustion, no error). Happened 4 times in Session #251. Explore: timeout watchdog, input chunking, or split prompts.

title: Install Codex plugin for Claude Code
description: Install openai/codex-plugin-cc. Cross-model adversarial review (/codex:adversarial-review), task delegation (/codex:rescue). Requires ChatGPT subscription (have it). Evaluate as complement to Gemini CLI contrarian challenges.
progress: Evaluated Session #261. Deferred installation — Windows blocked, Gemini may cover the gap. See project memory: project_codex_plugin_research.md.

title: /todo skill — cross-session todo management
description: JSONL-backed, interactive menu, context-aware capture, session hooks. Replace ad-hoc Next Session Goals tracking with persistent structured system.
progress: Discovery + planning complete. Built and tested.

title: GitHub Health skill — build from research
description: /github-health skill: resolution-first repo health assessment across 6 domains (security, actions, deps, config, release, insights). 21-agent L1 research complete with 100 claims. Next step is /deep-plan to produce implementation plan. Skill features interactive triage (Fix/Defer/Skip per item), direct API patches, PR creation for config fixes, and --quick mode for session-begin integration.
progress: Shipped in PR #492 — full skill with 7 phases, --quick mode, interactive triage. Merged Session #262.

title: Research-Discovery Standard — update plan with dashboard, skill drift, and /brainstorm
description: Three updates needed before RDS execution: (1) Add research/planning dashboard with document tracking, progress visibility, and status views. (2) Update deep-research agent orchestration sections — agent counts, invocation patterns, and circumstance-based dispatch have changed since original research (Session #238). (3) Integrate /brainstorm skill as new upstream phase before /deep-plan and /deep-research in the discovery pipeline. Plan at .planning/research-discovery-standard/PLAN.md is outdated on these points.
progress: Changes identified, plan updates not yet made. Now includes /brainstorm skill integration (new upstream phase before /deep-plan and /deep-research). Moved to in-progress Session #263.

title: Learnings analysis skill — research mitigation effectiveness
description: Create a skill that analyzes the full learnings ecosystem: 356+ documented patterns, 37 automated enforcements, 444 total in CODE_PATTERNS.md, learning-routes.jsonl, and review archives. Skill should: (1) assess which mitigation methods are working (pattern checker, hooks, CI gates, behavioral guardrails), (2) identify what's failing (4 patterns with 3+ recurrences, 15.8% automation coverage), (3) propose improved methods — automation candidates, better enforcement scaffolds, or new approaches. Builds on existing analyze-learning-effectiveness.js and LEARNING_METRICS.md infrastructure. Needs /deep-research first to survey the full system before planning.
progress: Research in progress in separate worktree.

title: Audit .gitignore coverage
description: Review all .gitignore rules against actual repo contents. Verify sensitive files (secrets, state, temp), build artifacts, and generated files are properly ignored. Check for over-ignoring (useful files accidentally excluded) or under-ignoring (files that should be excluded but aren't).
progress: Addressed in PR #498 R1 — volatile state files un-tracked, .gitignore updated.

title: Claude Code OS — multi-stage research initiative
description: Extract the battle-tested infrastructure from SoNash (65+ skills, 57 agents, 14 pre-commit checks, pattern registry, TDMS, convergence loops, CANON standards, etc.) into a project-agnostic portable operating system for Claude Code. This is the user's primary long-term vision — SoNash is the proving ground, the OS is the product. Requires extensive multi-stage research: (1) inventory what's portable vs app-specific, (2) define the OS architecture and layering, (3) dependency mapping between components, (4) extraction strategy and repo structure, (5) onboarding/bootstrap experience design. Eventually ports to its own repo. No timeline yet — research-first.

title: deep-research metadata gap — fix pipeline + audit all research
description: Phase 2 synthesizer writes metadata.json with early counts (agentCount, claimCount, findingsFilesCompleted). Phases 2.5-3.97 (verification, challenges, dispute resolution, gap pursuit, final re-synthesis) add many more claims and agents. The final synthesizer (Phase 3.97) does NOT reconcile core metadata fields — only adds synthesis-specific fields. Result: metadata.json and RESEARCH_OUTPUT.md Section 7.1 ('Missing Agent Findings') show stale/inaccurate data. TWO-PART FIX: (1) Fix the deep-research pipeline so final synthesizer updates core metadata fields. (2) Audit ALL existing .research/ outputs to identify which have stale metadata.
progress: 22-step plan executed across Sessions #257-259. Pipeline fixed (synthesizer agents updated). All 10 research outputs remediated + verified (51 PASS, 0 FAIL). 8-agent verification sweep + 3-agent impact analysis confirmed no completed implementations were built on wrong data.

title: Internal testing suite — processes, files, and workflows
description: Brainstorm a testing framework for validating internal infrastructure: processes (session lifecycle, PR review pipeline, debt intake, consolidation), files (JSONL data integrity, cross-doc dependencies, state file consistency), and workflows (skill invocation chains, hook pipelines, agent orchestration). Distinct from app-level tests (3564 node:test suite) — this targets the AI operational layer. Could leverage existing health scripts as a foundation and fill gaps where manual /system-test is the only coverage.

title: Alert refresh mechanisms — surface unacknowledged alerts outside /alerts
description: Investigate ways to surface unacknowledged alerts at different intervals beyond the current /alerts skill invocation. Current state: alerts surface at session-start (hook) and on-demand (/alerts). Gap: long sessions can go hours without seeing new warnings. Explore options like periodic mid-session reminders (e.g., after N tool calls or M minutes), post-commit alert checks, integration into /todo or /checkpoint flows, statusline indicators, or a lightweight alert pulse that fires at configurable intervals without the full /alerts overhead.

title: Orphan file/script/action search and repair
description: Build a systematic scan for orphaned assets across the repo: (1) scripts in scripts/ not called by package.json, hooks, or other scripts, (2) files referenced nowhere, (3) GitHub Actions/workflows not triggered by any path/event, (4) hook handlers not registered in .husky/ or .claude/hooks, (5) stale state/data files no longer read. Produce a report with repair actions per item: delete, archive, or wire up. Context: Session #263 doc-index check surfaced 1879 orphaned docs — orphan problem likely extends well beyond docs.
progress: Scanner done (428 findings, 110 resolved). npm run orphans:detect.

title: Create startup-help.md — all `claude --*` startup CLI options
description: Document every Claude Code CLI startup flag from `claude --help` with purpose, examples, and use-cases. Covers model selection, resume/continue, worktree (-w), fast mode, permission modes, print mode, allowed tools, MCP config, session options, etc. Goal: discoverable quick reference without running --help each time. Store at a logical location (likely docs/ or .claude/). Keep updated as CC versions add flags.
progress: Shipped as docs/HELP.md (528 lines) in commit 8c9ca3a8.

title: Website analysis skill — deep-research + build
description: Build /website-analysis skill for crawling and analyzing external websites. Deep-research in progress (brainstorm complete) in worktree-webcrawler. After research completes, proceed to /deep-plan and implementation.
progress: Skills built (website-analysis + website-synthesis). Deep-plan + skill-audit complete.

title: Synthesis adoption skill — drive /repo-synthesis + /website-synthesis usage
description: Build a skill that orchestrates the adoption pipeline for synthesis outputs. Context: 6 repos analyzed (v4.2 baselines ready), website-analysis/synthesis skills shipped, and the analysis-synthesis-comparison deep-research (60 claims) identified schema drift as critical and surfaced convergence opportunities. The convergence commit (5ab6b97) aligned shared conventions, Zod schemas, and 20 decisions. Next step is a skill that makes it easy to run synthesis across accumulated analyses, surface cross-repo/cross-site patterns, and act on findings. Could include: batch synthesis orchestration, adoption tracking (which analyses have been synthesized), gap detection (analyses missing prerequisites), and output routing (where findings land — TDMS, ROADMAP, memory).
